info.name: DocuWare
info.description:
**Last Updated: 9/04/2024**

**Summary:**

This collection contains examples of requests to the DocuWare Platform Services API.

**Getting Started:**

Getting started with the DocuWare Postman collection is easy and simply requires a small amount of up-front configuration.

1. Set the current environment to "DocuWare"
    
    1. If you do not have the "DocuWare" environment listed as one of your selections from the environments drop down, verify that you have imported the sample environment file that was included with the DocuWare Postman collection.
        
2. Set up the initial environment variable values.
    
    1. The following environment values should be set based on the DocuWare system you will be executing the API requests against.
        
        1. DocumentDateField - The name (ID) of the field that holds the document date in the target file cabinet.
            
        2. DocumentTrayName - This is the name of the document tray you want to use for testing. If left blank, the default document tray is used.
            
        3. DocumentTypeField - The name (ID) of the field that holds the document type in the target file cabinet.
            
        4. DocumentTypeValue - This is the value that will be used in this field (IE. Storing, Searching, etc.).
            
        5. DocumentTypeValueUpdated - This is the value that will be used when this index field is updated.
            
        6. FileCabinetName - This is the name of the file cabinet to be used for testing. If left empty, the first file cabinet returned will be used.
            
        7. Filename - This is the name of the file that will be used for storing new documents.
            
        8. Password - This should be the password for the user that you are connecting to DocuWare as.
            
        9. Platform - This should be set to '/DocuWare/Platform'. If it is not, ensure that it is.
            
        10. ServerUrl - This should be the address of your DocuWare system (IE. [https://your-system.docuware.cloud](https://your-system.docuware.cloud))
            
        11. Username - This should be the name of the user that you will be connecting to DocuWare as.
            
        12. WindowsUsername - This is the username for the Windows user that is used for Windows Authentication. It should be provided in the format of "\\"
            
        13. WindowsUserPassword - This is the password for the Windows user that is necessary for Windows Authentication,.
            
3. Authenticate with DocuWare
    
    1. Once the environment variables have been configured, you need to obtain an access token for making requests to DocuWare.
        
        1. Navigate to the following folder: DocuWare -> Authentication -> OAuth
            
        2. Execute the requests in this folder in the order indicated by the name
            
            1. 1\. Get Responsibile Identity Service
                
            2. 2\. Get Identity Service Configuration
                
            3. 3a - 3d - Execute one of these requests based on the information you have available.
                
    2. Notes:
        
        1. Step 3 is what provides the access token (which is saved in the 'AccessToken' environment variable). This token has a limited lifespan. If it expires, you only need to run the request in step 3 again (you do not need to executed the first 2 requests).
            
        2. It should also be noted that DocuWare only supports certain grant types for authentication. Supported types are:
            
            1. Username & Password
                
            2. DocuWare Token
                
            3. Trusted User Login (On premises only)
                
            4. Windows Login (On premises only)
                
4. Executing DocuWare operational requests
    
    1. Once you have obtained an access token, you can start making requests to DocuWare. It should be noted that some requests are dependent on other requests. For example, you can't download a document unless you have retrieved the ID of the file cabinet you are downloading from and the ID of the document you are downloading. Before a request is executed, it will check the relevant environment, and throw an error if they are empty.
        

Common Scenarios:

- Searching a File Cabinet
    
    - Authenticate with DocuWare (as outlined in step 3)
        
    - Get all File Cabinets: General -> Organization -> Get all File Cabinets and Document Trays
        
        - If you filled out the "FileCabinetName" environment variable as outlined in step 1, then this will retrieve the ID of that File Cabinet and save it in an environment variable for use in requests that need it.
            
    - Retrieve required dialogs: File Cabinets -> Dialogs -> Get all Dialogs
        
    - Search for documents: File Cabinets -> Search -> Get Documents from a File Cabinet
        
- Uploading a Document
    
    - Authenticate with DocuWare (as outlined in step 3)
        
    - Get all File Cabinets: General -> Organization -> Get all File Cabinets and Document Trays
        
        - If you filled out the "FileCabinetName" environment variable as outlined in step 1, then this will retrieve the ID of that File Cabinet and save it in an environment variable for use in requests that need it.
            
    - Retrieve required dialogs: File Cabinets -> Dialogs -> Get all Dialogs
        
    - Upload a document: File Cabinets -> Upload -> Create a Data Record & Upload File
        
- Downloading a Document
    
    - Authenticate with DocuWare (as outlined in step 3)
        
    - Get all File Cabinets: General -> Organization -> Get all File Cabinets and Document Trays
        
        - If you filled out the "FileCabinetName" environment variable as outlined in step 1, then this will retrieve the ID of that File Cabinet and save it in an environment variable for use in requests that need it.
            
    - Retrieve required dialogs: File Cabinets -> Dialogs -> Get all Dialogs
        
    - Find a document to download: File Cabinets -> Search -> Get Documents from a File Cabinet
        
        - The first X number of documents (where X represents the 'Count' parameter) will be returned and the ID of the first document will be saved in the 'DocID' environment variable.
            
    - Download the specified document: Documents -> Download -> Download Document
        
        - This will download the first document returned from the search in the previous step.
            
- Update Index Field(s)
    
    - Authenticate with DocuWare (as outlined in step 3)
        
    - Get all File Cabinets: General -> Organization -> Get all File Cabinets and Document Trays
        
        - If you filled out the "FileCabinetName" environment variable as outlined in step 1, then this will retrieve the ID of that File Cabinet and save it in an environment variable for use in requests that need it.
            
    - Find a document to download: File Cabinets -> Search -> Get Documents from a File Cabinet
        
        - The first X number of documents (where X represents the 'Count' parameter) will be returned and the ID of the first document will be saved in the 'DocID' environment variable.
            
    - Update the index values for the specified document: Documents -> Update Index Values -> Update Index Values
        
        - Index fields for the specified document will be updated according to what is defined in the request body. By default, the request body uses index field information that was configured when the collection was setup.
            

Notes:

- It should be noted that every effort has been made to ensure that each request does a pre-execution check to ensure the necessary environment variables are present and not empty. If they are not present or are empty, an error will be thrown. In that case, check to ensure that the environment variable does exist and is not empty (it's possible you may need to execute another request before executing the one you are trying).

request.description:
This API endpoint retrieves information about the Identity Service in the DocuWare Platform. It sends an HTTP GET request to the URL `{{ServerUrl}}/DocuWare/Platform/Home/IdentityServiceInfo`.

### Request

The request does not require any parameters.

### Response

The response will be in JSON format and will have the following properties:

- `IdentityServiceUrl`: The URL of the Identity Service in the DocuWare Platform.
- `RefreshTokenSupported`: A boolean value indicating whether the Identity Service supports refresh tokens.
    

Example response:

``` antlr4
{
    "IdentityServiceUrl": "",
    "RefreshTokenSupported": true
}

 ```

exec: throw new Error("IdentityServiceUrl is empty or does not exist. Please ensure it exists and run the '1.  Get Responsible Identity Service' request.");

request.description:
This API endpoint retrieves the OpenID configuration for the Identity Service. The OpenID configuration provides important information about the Identity Service, such as the endpoints and supported features.

To retrieve the OpenID configuration, send an HTTP GET request to `{{IdentityServiceUrl}}/.well-known/openid-configuration`.

The last execution of this request returned a response with a status code of 200. The response body contains the following information:

- `issuer`: The issuer URL of the Identity Service.
- `jwks_uri`: The URL to retrieve the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) for verifying JSON Web Tokens (JWTs).
- `authorization_endpoint`: The URL to initiate the authorization process.
- `token_endpoint`: The URL to exchange an authorization code for an access token.
- `userinfo_endpoint`: The URL to retrieve user information.
- `end_session_endpoint`: The URL to end the user's session.
- `check_session_iframe`: The URL of the check session iframe.
- `revocation_endpoint`: The URL to revoke an access token.
- `introspection_endpoint`: The URL to introspect an access token.
- `device_authorization_endpoint`: The URL for device authorization.
- `backchannel_authentication_endpoint`: The URL for backchannel authentication.
- `frontchannel_logout_supported`: Indicates whether frontchannel logout is supported.
- `frontchannel_logout_session_supported`: Indicates whether frontchannel logout with session ID is supported.
- `backchannel_logout_supported`: Indicates whether backchannel logout is supported.
- `backchannel_logout_session_supported`: Indicates whether backchannel logout with session ID is supported.
- `scopes_supported`: The supported scopes for requesting access to resources.
- `claims_supported`: The supported claims that can be requested in an ID token or userinfo response.
- `grant_types_supported`: The supported grant types for obtaining an access token.
- `response_types_supported`: The supported response types for the authorization process.
- `response_modes_supported`: The supported response modes for the authorization process.
- `token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported`: The supported authentication methods for the token endpoint.
- `id_token_signing_alg_values_supported`: The supported signing algorithms for ID tokens.
- `subject_types_supported`: The supported subject types for ID tokens.
- `code_challenge_methods_supported`: The supported code challenge methods for PKCE.
- `request_parameter_supported`: Indicates whether the request parameter is supported.
- `request_object_signing_alg_values_supported`: The supported signing algorithms for request objects.
- `prompt_values_supported`: The supported prompt values for the authorization process.
- `authorization_response_iss_parameter_supported`: Indicates whether the `iss` parameter is supported in authorization responses.
- `backchannel_token_delivery_modes_supported`: The supported token delivery modes for backchannel authentication.
- `backchannel_user_code_parameter_supported`: Indicates whether the `user_code` parameter is supported for backchannel authentication.
- `dpop_signing_alg_values_supported`: The supported signing algorithms for DPoP tokens.
- `windows_auth_endpoint`: The URL for Windows authentication.
    

Please refer to the Identity Service documentation for more information on how to use these endpoints and features.

request.description:
This API endpoint is used to request a token from the server. The token can be used for authentication and authorization purposes. 

To request a token, send a POST request to the `{{TokenEndpoint}}` URL.

### Request Parameters

The request does not require any specific parameters in the body or headers.

### Response

The server will respond with a JSON object containing the following properties:

- `access_token`: The generated access token.
- `expires_in`: The expiration time of the token in seconds.
- `token_type`: The type of the token.
- `scope`: The scope of the token.

Example response:

```json
{
    "access_token": "",
    "expires_in": 0,
    "token_type": "",
    "scope": ""
}
```

Please note that the values of the properties in the response may vary depending on the server configuration.

item.description:
This folder provides examples of requests for authenticating with DocuWare using OAuth. The requests are listed in an order indicating the sequence of requests necessary to complete the authentication process.

It should be noted, that at this time, DocuWare only supports the following grant types for retreiving an access token:

- Username & Password
    
- DocuWare Token
    
- Trusted User Login
    
- Windows Login
    
- Login Token
    

No other grant types are currently supported.

This folder contains sample requests for authenticating with DocuWare. At this time, DocuWare supports two methods of authentication: Cookie and OAuth. It should be noted that Cookie Authentication is being phased out. As such, it is recommended that you use OAuth for any future projects.

item.name: General
Organization

request.description:
### POST /{{Platform}}/Organization/LoginToken

This endpoint is used to log in to the organization and obtain a login token.

#### Request Body

- `TargetProducts` (array of strings, optional): An array of target products.
- `Usage` (string, optional): The usage of the login token.
- `Lifetime` (string, optional): The lifetime of the login token.
    

#### Response

The response will be an HTML document with the status code 200. The HTML document returned contains the login page with various form fields for providing username, password, and other login options.

This HTTP GET request is used to retrieve a list of organizations from the server. The request should be made to the following endpoint: `{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/Organizations`.

### Request Parameters

This request does not require any specific parameters to be included in the URL or body.

### Response

The response to this request will have a status code of 200. The body of the response will be in JSON format and will contain an array of organizations. Each organization object will have the following properties:

- `Links`: An array of links associated with the organization. Each link object will have a `rel` (relation) and `href` (URL) property.
- `Name`: The name of the organization.
- `Id`: The unique identifier of the organization.
- `Guid`: The globally unique identifier (GUID) of the organization.
    

### Example Response

Here is an example response for this request:

```
{
  "Organization": [
    {
      "Links": [
        {
          "rel": "",
          "href": ""
        }
      ],
      "Name": "",
      "Id": "",
      "Guid": ""
    }
  ]
}

 ```

Please note that the actual response may contain different values for the `Links`, `Name`, `Id`, and `Guid` properties.

request.description:
This API endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of file cabinets for a specific organization. The file cabinets represent a collection of files or documents stored in the system.

To retrieve the list of file cabinets, make an HTTP GET request to the following URL: `{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets?orgid={{OrgId}}`.

The response to this request will be a JSON object with a `FileCabinet` property. The `FileCabinet` property contains an array of file cabinet objects, each representing a file cabinet in the system.

Each file cabinet object has the following properties:

- `Links`: An array of links associated with the file cabinet. Each link object has a `rel` (relation) and `href` (URL) property.
- `Color`: The color associated with the file cabinet.
- `Name`: The name of the file cabinet.
- `Id`: The unique identifier of the file cabinet.
- `IsBasket`: A boolean value indicating whether the file cabinet is a basket.
- `Usable`: A boolean value indicating whether the file cabinet is usable.
- `Default`: A boolean value indicating whether the file cabinet is the default file cabinet.
- `VersionManagement`: The version management setting for the file cabinet.
- `WindowsExplorerClientAccess`: A boolean value indicating whether the file cabinet can be accessed using the Windows Explorer client.
- `AddIndexEntriesInUpperCase`: A boolean value indicating whether index entries should be added in uppercase.
- `DocumentAuditingEnabled`: A boolean value indicating whether document auditing is enabled for the file cabinet.
- `HasFullTextSupport`: A boolean value indicating whether the file cabinet has full-text support.
    

Please note that the response may include additional properties not mentioned here, but these are the properties that are guaranteed to be present.

Here is an example response for this request:

``` json
{
  "FileCabinet": [
    {
      "Links": [
        {
          "rel": "",
          "href": ""
        }
      ],
      "Color": "",
      "Name": "",
      "Id": "",
      "IsBasket": true,
      "Usable": true,
      "Default": true,
      "VersionManagement": "",
      "WindowsExplorerClientAccess": true,
      "AddIndexEntriesInUpperCase": true,
      "DocumentAuditingEnabled": true,
      "HasFullTextSupport": true
    }
  ]
}

 ```

Please note that the values in the example response are placeholders and may not reflect the actual values returned by the API.

request.description:
This API endpoint allows you to retrieve information about the users in an organization.

To make a request, send an HTTP GET request to the following URL:

```
{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/Organization/Users

 ```

### Response

The API will respond with a status code of 200 if the request is successful. The response body will contain an array of user objects, with each object representing a user in the organization.

Each user object will have the following properties:

- `IsHighSecurity` (boolean): Indicates whether the user has high security settings enabled.
- `EMail` (string): The email address associated with the user.
- `DefaultWebBasket` (string): The default document tray for the user.
    
- `OutOfOffice` (object): An object representing the out-of-office status for the user.
    - `IsOutOfOffice` (boolean): Indicates whether the user is currently out of office.
    - `StartDateTime` (string): The start date and time of the out-of-office period.
    - `StartDateTimeSpecified` (boolean): Indicates whether the start date and time is specified.
    - `EndDateTimeSpecified` (boolean): Indicates whether the end date and time is specified.
- `Links` (array): An array of link objects associated with the user.
    - `rel` (string): The relationship of the link.
    - `href` (string): The URL of the link.
- `Id` (string): The unique identifier of the user.
- `Name` (string): The name of the user.
- `FirstName` (string): The first name of the user.
- `LastName` (string): The last name of the user.
- `DBName` (string): The name of the database associated with the user.
- `Active` (boolean): Indicates whether the user is active.
    

Please note that the response may contain multiple user objects, depending on the number of users in the organization.

query.description: The ID of the user that you want to get.

request.description:
This HTTP GET request is used to retrieve information about a user in an organization based on their user ID (as returned in the response from "Get All Users"). The request is sent to the server URL and platform specified in the request URL.

To retrieve information about a specific user, provide the user ID as a query parameter in the request URL. The user ID is passed as the value for the "userId" parameter.

The execution of this request returns a response with a status code of 200, indicating a successful request. The response body contains the following information:

- "IsHighSecurity": A boolean value indicating whether the user has high security settings enabled.
- "EMail": The email address associated with the user.
- "DefaultWebBasket": The default document tray for the user.
    
- "OutOfOffice": An object containing information about the user's out-of-office status. It includes:
    - "IsOutOfOffice": A boolean value indicating whether the user is currently out of office.
    - "StartDateTime": The start date and time of the user's out-of-office period.
    - "StartDateTimeSpecified": A boolean value indicating whether the start date and time is specified.
    - "EndDateTimeSpecified": A boolean value indicating whether the end date and time is specified.
- "RegionalSettings": An object containing the user's regional settings. It includes:
    - "Language": The language preference of the user.
    - "Culture": The culture preference of the user.
- "Links": An array of objects representing links related to the user. Each object includes:
    - "rel": The relationship of the link.
    - "href": The URL of the link.
- "Id": The ID of the user.
- "Name": The name of the user.
- "DBName": The name of the user's database.
- "Active": A boolean value indicating whether the user is active.
    

Please note that specific values such as names and emails may not be included in this description for privacy reasons.

To use this endpoint, make an HTTP GET request to the specified URL, providing the user ID as a query parameter. The server will respond with the requested user information.

query.description: Specify the ID of the role that you want the associated users from.

This value (true/false) specifies whether the return results should contain just users directly associated to the role or users also contained in groups associated to the role. If left empty, it defaults to false.

request.description:
This endpoint makes an HTTP GET request to retrieve the users associated with a specific role in the organization. The request includes the RoleId as a query parameter and an optional parameter IncludeGroupUsers.

### Request Parameters

- RoleId (query parameter): The unique identifier of the role for which the associated users are to be retrieved.
- IncludeGroupUsers (optional query parameter): Indicates whether to include group users in the response.
    

### Response

Upon a successful execution, the endpoint returns a status code of 200 and a JSON response with an array of User objects. Each User object contains information such as IsHighSecurity, EMail, DefaultWebBasket, OutOfOffice status, Id, Name, DBName, and Active status.

Example response:

``` json
{
    "User": [
        {
            "IsHighSecurity": true,
            "EMail": "",
            "DefaultWebBasket": "",
            "OutOfOffice": {
                "IsOutOfOffice": true,
                "StartDateTime": "",
                "StartDateTimeSpecified": true,
                "EndDateTimeSpecified": true
            },
            "Id": "",
            "Name": "",
            "DBName": "",
            "Active": true
        }
    ]
}

 ```

item.name: Create/Update Users

request.description:
This API endpoint allows you to add user information to an organization on the specified platform. It is an HTTP POST request to the URL `{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/Organization/UserInfo`.

### Request Parameters

The request URL should include the following parameters:

- `ServerUrl`: The URL of the server hosting the file cabinet.
- `Platform`: The platform on which the file cabinet is hosted.
    

The request body needs to contain an object in this format:

``` json
{
    "Name": "Peter Engineering",
    "DbName": "PENGINE",
    "Email": "peter@peters-engineering.test",
    "NetworkId": "",
    "Password": "YOURPASSWORD"
}

 ```

This object specifies information about the new user that will be created in DocuWare. Here are the details for each field in the object:

- "Name" is a string that represents the name of the new user.
- "DbName" is a string that represents the name of the database configured in DocuWare for the new user.
- "Email" is a string that represents the email address of the new user.
- "NetworkId" is a string that represents the network ID of the new user.
- "Password" is a string that represents the password of the new user.  
    Note that the NetworkId field is optional, and can be left blank if no value is available.
    

### Response

After the execution of this request, the server returns a response with a status code of 200. The response body contains the following information:

- `IsHighSecurity`: A boolean value indicating whether the user has high security settings enabled.
- `EMail`: The email address of the user.
- `DefaultWebBasket`: The default web basket associated with the user.
- `OutOfOffice`: An object containing information about the user's out-of-office settings. It includes the following properties:
    - `IsOutOfOffice`: A boolean value indicating whether the user is currently out of office.
    - `StartDateTimeSpecified`: A boolean value indicating whether the start date and time for the out-of-office period is specified.
    - `EndDateTimeSpecified`: A boolean value indicating whether the end date and time for the out-of-office period is specified.
- `RegionalSettings`: An object containing regional settings for the user. The specific properties and their values are not provided in the response.
- `Links`: An array of objects representing links associated with the user. Each object in the array includes the following properties:
    - `rel`: The relationship of the link.
    - `href`: The URL of the link.
- `Id`: The unique identifier of the user.
- `Name`: The name of the user.
- `DBName`: The name of the database associated with the user.
- `Active`: A boolean value indicating whether the user is active.
    

Please note that the specific values of certain properties, such as names and email addresses, are not included in the response for privacy reasons.

For more details on how to use this API endpoint and the available request parameters, please refer to the API documentation.

### Add Organization User Information

This API endpoint allows you to add user information for an organization on the specified platform.

#### Request Body

- `Id` (string, required): The unique identifier for the user.
    
- `Actve` (boolean, optional): Indicates whether the user is active.
    
- `FirstName` (string, optional): The first name of the user.
- `LastName` (string, optional): The last name of the user.
- `Salutation` (string, optional): The salutation for the user.
- `Name` (string, optional): The name of the user.
- `Email` (string, optional): The email address of the user.
- `OutOfOffice` (object, optional): Information about the user's out of office status.
    - `IsOutOfOffice` (string, optional): Indicates whether the user is out of office.
    - `StartDateTime` (string, optional): The start date and time of the out of office period.
    - `EndDateTime` (string, optional): The end date and time of the out of office period.

#### Response

Upon successful execution, the API returns a status code of 200 and a JSON response with the following fields:

- `IsHighSecurity` (boolean): Indicates whether high security settings are enabled.
- `EMail` (string): The email address of the user.
- `DefaultWebBasket` (string): The default web basket for the user.

request.description:
This API endpoint is used to retrieve information about a specific file cabinet. It is an HTTP GET request that should be made to the following URL: `{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{FileCabinetId}}`.

The response from the execution of this request has a status code of 200, indicating a successful response. The response body contains the following information:

- `Links`: An array of objects representing links related to the file cabinet. Each object has a `rel` (relation) and `href` (URL) property.
- `Fields`: An array of objects representing the fields/columns of the file cabinet. Each field object contains various properties such as `TableFieldColumns`, `UsedAsDocumentName`, `Links`, `Scope`, `DWFieldType`, `Length`, `DBFieldName`, `DisplayName`, `DropLeadingZero`, `DropLeadingBlanks`, `NotEmpty`, and `Precision`.
- `Rights`: An array of strings representing the rights associated with the file cabinet.
- `ExtendedUserRights`: An object containing extended user rights information. An example of the response is a property `AllowViewAuditData` set to `true`.
    
- `Color`: A string representing the color associated with the file cabinet.
- `Name`: A string representing the name of the file cabinet.
- `Id`: A string representing the unique identifier of the file cabinet.
- `IsBasket`: A boolean value indicating whether the file cabinet is a basket or not.
- `Usable`: A boolean value indicating whether the file cabinet is usable or not.
- `Default`: A boolean value indicating whether the file cabinet is the default file cabinet or not.
- `VersionManagement`: A string representing the version management setting of the file cabinet.
- `WindowsExplorerClientAccess`: A boolean value indicating whether the file cabinet can be accessed via Windows Explorer or not.
- `AddIndexEntriesInUpperCase`: A boolean value indicating whether the index entries should be added in uppercase or not.
- `DocumentAuditingEnabled`: A boolean value indicating whether document auditing is enabled for the file cabinet or not.
- `HasFullTextSupport`: A boolean value indicating whether the file cabinet has full-text support or not.
    

Please note that the specific values (if any) in the response have been omitted for security and privacy reasons.

Please refer to the API documentation for more details on how to use this endpoint and the meaning of each property in the response.

- `Type`: The type of the dialog.

The response for this request includes various properties related to the document. Here are some examples of the response properties:

- `Fields`: An array of fields associated with the document. Each field object includes information such as field name, label, nullability, read-only status, and system field indicator.
- `Flags`: An object containing various flags indicating properties of the document, such as whether it is a cold document, a database record, checked out, copy-right protected, voice available, has appended documents, protected, deleted, or an email.
- `Links`: An array of links associated with the document. Each link object includes information about the link's relationship and href.
- `Sections`: An array of sections within the document. Each section object includes information such as signature status, links, section ID, content type, page count, file size, original file name, content modification status, and text annotation availability.
- `ContentType`: The content type of the document.
- `HaveMoreTotalPages`: A flag indicating whether there are more pages in the document.
- `HasTextAnnotation`: A flag indicating whether the document has text annotations.
- `HasXmlDigitalSignatures`: A flag indicating whether the document has XML digital signatures.
- `TotalPages`: The total number of pages in the document.
- `Id`: The ID of the document.
- `Title`: The title of the document.
- `LastModified`: The last modified date of the document.
- `CreatedAt`: The creation date of the document.
- `FileSize`: The file size of the document.
- `SectionCount`: The number of sections in the document.
- `IntellixTrust`: The IntellixTrust value of the document.
- `VersionStatus`: The version status of the document.
- `FileCabinetId`: The ID of the file cabinet containing the document.
    

Please note that the response example provided in the question is not a valid JSON response and may not accurately represent the actual response structure.

request.description:
This API endpoint allows you to query a specific file cabinet in the system and retrieve information about the documents stored within it. The request is an HTTP POST request sent to the server URL, with the specific platform and file cabinet ID provided in the URL path. The query is performed using a dialog expression, which can be customized to filter and sort the results.

### Request Parameters

To execute the query, you need to provide the following parameters in the request URL:

- Count: Specifies the maximum number of results to return. In the last execution, the value was set to 2.
- Fields: Specifies the fields to include in the response. Only the DOCUMENT_TYPE field was included.
    
- SortOrder: Specifies the sorting order for the results. The results are sorted by DOCUMENT_TYPE in ascending order.
    

Additionally, you need to provide the DialogId parameter in the URL, which is used to identify the specific dialog to use for the query. This value should be replaced with the actual search dialog ID.

The request body needs to contain an object in this format:

``` json
{
 "Condition":[ {"DBName":"COMPANY_NAME", "Value":["US-Steel"]},
               {"DBName":"DOCUMENT_DATE", "Value":["2010-03-01", "2010-03-30"]}
             ],
 "Operation":"Or"
}

 ```

This object specifies the search criteria to be used when executing the search dialog. Here are the details for each field in the object:

- "Condition" is an array of objects that specifies the search criteria to be used for the search dialog. Each object in the array represents a separate search condition, and contains the following fields:
- "DBName" is a string that represents the name of the database field being searched.
- "Value" is an array of strings that represents the search value(s) to be used for the specified database field. Note that multiple search values provided for a single database field are interpreted as an OR operation.
- "Operation" is a string that specifies the boolean operation to be used when evaluating the search conditions in the "Condition" array. In this case, the value "Or" indicates that the search dialog should return results that match any of the specified search conditions.  
    Note that the specific fields used in the "Condition" array will depend on the fields available in the database being searched, as well as the specific search dialog being used.
    

### Response

```

Each object contains information about the document, such as the file cabinet ID, fields, flags indicating various properties of the document, links to related resources, sections, content type, page count, and version status.

request.description:
This API endpoint allows you to query a specific file cabinet's dialog expression by providing the file cabinet ID and search dialog ID. The request is an HTTP POST request to the URL `{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{FileCabinetId}}/Query/DialogExpression?DialogId={{SearchDialogId}}`.

### Request Parameters

The request URL should include the following parameters:

- ServerUrl: The URL of the server hosting the file cabinet.
- Platform: The platform on which the file cabinet is hosted.
- FileCabinetId: The ID of the file cabinet containing the document.
- SearchDialogId: The ID of the search dialog this query will use
    

The request body needs to contain an object in this format:

``` json
{
    "Condition": [
        {
            "DBName": "COMPANY_NAME",
            "Value": [
                "US-Steel"
            ]
        },
        {
            "DBName": "DOCUMENT_DATE",
            "Value": [
                "2010-03-01",
                "{{CurrentDate}}"
            ]
        }
    ],
    "AdditionalCabinets": [
        "0242b887-0261-473d-aa59-96eb5cd5f709",
    ],
    "SortOrder": [
        {
            "Field": "DOCUMENT_TYPE",
            "Direction": "Desc"
        }
    ],
    "ForceRefresh": true,
    "Operation": "And",
    "AdditionalResultFields": [],
    "Start": 0,
    "Count": 100,
    "IncludeSuggestions": false
}

 ```

This object specifies the search criteria to be used when querying for a document in DocuWare. Here are the details for each field in the object:

- "Condition" is an array of objects that specifies the search criteria to be used for the query. Each object in the array represents a separate search condition, and contains the following fields:
- "DBName" is a string that represents the name of the database field being searched.
- "Value" is an array of strings that represents the search value(s) to be used for the specified database field. Note that multiple search values provided for a single database field are interpreted as an OR operation.
- "AdditionalCabinets" is an array of strings that represents the ID(s) of any additional FileCabinets to search, in addition to the primary FileCabinet specified in the query endpoint URL.
- "SortOrder" is an array of objects that specifies the sorting criteria to be used for the query. Each object in the array represents a separate field to sort by, and contains those fields.
- "Field" is a string that represents the name of the database field to sort by.
- "Direction" is a string that specifies the direction of the sort, either "Asc" for ascending or "Desc" for descending.
- "ForceRefresh" is a boolean flag that indicates whether to force a refresh of the cached search results.
- "Operation" is a string that specifies the boolean operation to be used when evaluating the search conditions in the "Condition" array. In this case, the value "And" indicates that the query should return results that match all of the specified search conditions.
- "AdditionalResultFields" is an array of strings that specifies any additional database fields to include in the query results.
- "Start" is an integer that represents the index of the first document to include in the query results.
- "Count" is an integer that represents the maximum number of documents to include in the query results.
- "IncludeSuggestions" is a boolean flag that indicates whether to include query suggestions in the response.  
    Note that the specific fields used in the "Condition" array will depend on the fields available in the database being searched.
    

### Response

The response returned by the execution of this endpoint has a status code of 200. The response body contains the following information:

- `Count`: An object that indicates if there are more results available and if the query exceeded the limit. The `Value` property represents the number of items returned.
- `Items`: An array of objects representing the queried dialog expressions. Each item contains the following properties:
    - `FileCabinetId`: The ID of the file cabinet.
    - `Fields`: An array of objects representing the fields of the dialog expression. Each field object contains the following properties:
        - `SystemField`: Indicates if the field is a system field.
        - `FieldName`: The name of the field.
        - `FieldLabel`: The label of the field.
        - `IsNull`: Indicates if the field is null.
        - `ReadOnly`: Indicates if the field is read-only.
        - `Item`: The item value of the field.
        - `ItemElementName`: The element name of the item value.
    - `Flags`: An object that represents various flags associated with the dialog expression. The available flags are:
        - `IsCold`: Indicates if the dialog expression is cold.
        - `IsDBRecord`: Indicates if the dialog expression is a database record.
        - `IsCheckedOut`: Indicates if the dialog expression is checked out.
        - `IsCopyRightProtected`: Indicates if the dialog expression is copyright protected.
        - `IsVoiceAvailable`: Indicates if voice is available for the dialog expression.
        - `HasAppendedDocuments`: Indicates if the dialog expression has appended documents.
        - `IsProtected`: Indicates if the dialog expression is protected.
        - `IsDeleted`: Indicates if the dialog expression is deleted.
        - `IsEmail`: Indicates if the dialog expression is an email.
    - `Links`: An array of objects representing the links associated with the dialog expression. Each link object contains the following properties:
        - `rel`: The relationship of the link.
        - `href`: The URL of the link.
    - `Sections`: An array representing the sections of the dialog expression.
    - `ContentType`: The content type of the dialog expression.
    - `HaveMoreTotalPages`: Indicates if there are more total pages available.
    - `HasTextAnnotation`: Indicates if the dialog expression has text annotations.
    - `HasXmlDigitalSignatures`: Indicates if the dialog expression has XML digital signatures.
    - `AnnotationsPreview`: Indicates if the dialog expression has annotations preview.
    - `TotalPages`: The total number of pages.
    - `Id`: The ID of the dialog expression.
    - `Title`: The title of the dialog expression.
    - `LastModified`: The last modified date of the dialog expression.
    - `LastModifiedSpecified`: Indicates if the last modified date is specified.
    - `CreatedAt`: The creation date of the dialog expression.
    - `CreatedAtSpecified`: Indicates if the creation date is specified.
    - `FileSize`: The file size of the dialog expression.
    - `SectionCount`: The number of sections in the dialog expression.
    - `IntellixTrust`: The Intellix trust of the dialog expression.
    - `VersionStatus`: The version status of the dialog expression.

Please note that specific values are not included in this description for security and privacy reasons. Make sure to replace the placeholders in the request URL with the actual values when making the API call.

request.description:
This API endpoint is used to checkout a document from a file cabinet and save it to the file system. The document is identified by the `FileCabinetId` and `DocumentId` parameters in the URL.

To checkout a document, send an HTTP POST request to `{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{FileCabinetId}}Documents/{{DocumentId}}/CheckoutToFileSystem`.

The response to this request will have a status code of 200, indicating a successful checkout. The response body will contain a JSON object with a `Links` property. The `Links` property is an array of objects, where each object represents a link related to the checked out document. Each link object has two properties: `rel` and `href`. The `rel` property represents the relationship of the link, and the `href` property contains the URL of the link.

Here is an example response for this request:

``` json
{
  "Links": [
    {
      "rel": "",
      "href": ""
    }
  ]
}

 ```

Please note that the actual values for `rel` and `href` may vary depending on the specific document and file cabinet being checked out.

Notes:

- Version management must be enabled for the specified file cabinet.
- The generated file name for the checked out document will be contained in the "Content-Disposition" header. You must use this filename if storing the document to the file system.

This HTTP POST request is used to query a list of selected expressions from a specific file cabinet in the platform. The request is sent to the endpoint `{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{FileCabinetId}}/Query/SelectListExpression` with additional query parameters `DialogId` and `FieldName`.

### Request Parameters

The request URL should include the following parameters:

- ServerUrl: The URL of the server hosting the file cabinet.
- Platform: The platform on which the file cabinet is hosted.
- FileCabinetId: The ID of the file cabinet containing the document.
- SearchDialogId: The ID of the document from which the file should be downloaded.
    

The request body needs to contain an object in this format:

``` json
{
  "ValuePrefix": "",
  "Limit": 100,
  "Typed": true,
  "ExcludeExternal": true,
  "DialogExpression": {
    "Operation": "And",
    "Condition": [
      {
        "DBName": "DOCUMENT_TYPE",
        "Value": [
          "\"DocuWare\""
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}

 ```

This object specifies the search criteria to be used when querying for a document in DocuWare. Here are the details for each field in the object:  
This object specifies the parameters to be used when retrieving filtered select lists from DocuWare. Here are the details for each field in the object:

- "ValuePrefix" is a string that allows you to specify a prefix that the select list items must start with. For example, if "ValuePrefix" is set to "A", only select list items that start with the letter "A" will be returned.
- "Limit" is an integer that specifies the maximum number of select list items to return.
- "Typed" is a boolean flag that indicates whether to return select list values as pre-typed strings.
- "ExcludeExternal" is a boolean flag that indicates whether to exclude external select lists from the result set.
- "DialogExpression" is an object that specifies the data source and search criteria to be used for the select list. Here are the details for each field in the object:
- "Operation" is a string that specifies the boolean operation to be used when evaluating the search conditions in the "Condition" array. In this case, the value "And" indicates that the query should return results that match all of the specified search conditions.
- "Condition" is an array of objects that specifies the search conditions to be used for the select list. Each object in the array represents a separate search condition, and contains the following fields:
- "DBName" is a string that represents the name of the database field being searched.
- "Value" is an array of strings that represents the search value(s) to be used for the specified database field.  
    Note that the specific fields used in the "Condition" array will depend on the fields available in the database being searched.
    

### Response

The execution of this request returns a response with a status code of 200. The response body is as follows:

``` json
{
    "TypedValue": []
}

 ```

The `TypedValue` property in the response body is an array that represents the list of selected expressions returned by the query. In this case, the array is empty, indicating that no expressions were found matching the search criteria.

Please note that the actual values for `{{ServerUrl}}`, `{{Platform}}`, `{{FileCabinetId}}`, and `{{SearchDialogId}}` should be provided when making the request.

request.description:
This HTTP POST request is used to add a new section to a specific document in a file cabinet within a platform, identified by the provided FileCabinetId. The request includes the DocumentId as a query parameter. The payload contains the file to be added as a section.

The response to the request returns a status code of 200, indicating a successful operation. The response body includes information about the signature status, pages, thumbnails, links, ID, content type, page count, file size, original file name, content modification status, and text annotation details.

The "SignatureStatus" array indicates the signature status, while the "Pages" and "Thumbnails" objects provide details about the pages and thumbnails of the added section. The "Links" array contains related links, and additional information such as ID, content type, file size, original file name, content modification, and text annotation details are also included in the response.

### Replace Document Section

This endpoint allows you to replace a specific section with a new file in the specified document.

#### Request Parameters

- `FileCabinetId` (path): The unique identifier of the file cabinet.
- `SectionId` (path): The identifier of the section where the file will be added.
- `fileName` (query): The name of the file being added.
    

#### Request Body

The file to be added to the section.

#### Response

Upon a successful execution, the response will include the status code `200` and the content type `application/json`. The response body will contain information about the signature status, pages, thumbnails, links, file details, and annotations related to the added file.

This API endpoint is used to perform batch updates on the fields of multiple documents within a file cabinet. The HTTP POST request should be made to the following URL: `{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{FileCabinetId}}/Operations/BatchDocumentsUpdateFields`.

### Request Parameters

The request URL should include the following parameters:

- ServerUrl: The URL of the server hosting the file cabinet.
- Platform: The platform on which the file cabinet is hosted.
- FileCabinetId: The ID of the file cabinet containing the document.
    

The request body needs to contain an object in this format:

``` json
{
    "Source": {
        "$type": "BatchUpdateDocumentsSource",
        "Id": [
            309,
            310
        ]
    },
    "Data": {
        "Field": [
            {
                "FieldName": "DOCUMENT_TYPE",
                "Item": "Batch Update Test"
            }
        ],
        "StoreDialogId": "",
        "BatchSize": "100",
        "BreakOnError": false,
        "ForceUpdate": true
    }
}

 ```

This object specifies the parameters to be used when performing a batch update on multiple documents in DocuWare. Here are the details for each field in the object:

- "Source" is an object that specifies the source documents to be updated. Here are the details for each field in the object:
    
    - "$type" is a string that specifies the type of source documents being updated.
        
    - "Id" is an array of integers that represents the document IDs of the documents to be updated.
        
- "Data" is an object that specifies the updated data to be applied to each of the source documents. Here are the details for each field in the object:
    
    - "Field" is an array of objects that represents the updated field data to be applied to each of the source documents. Each object in the array contains the following fields:
        
        - "FieldName" is a string that represents the name of the field to be updated.
            
        - "Item" is a string that represents the new value to be assigned to the field.
            
    - "StoreDialogId" is a string that represents the ID of the store dialog to be used when updating the documents.
        
    - "BatchSize" is a string that represents the maximum number of documents to be updated in a single batch.
        
    - "BreakOnError" is a boolean flag that indicates whether to stop the batch update process if an error is encountered.
        
    - "ForceUpdate" is a boolean flag that indicates whether to force the update of all documents, even if no changes were made to their field data.
        

### Response

The response received from the execution of this request has a status code of 200, indicating a successful operation. The response body contains the following information:

item.name: Batch Update Index Fields By Search

request.description:
This API endpoint allows you to perform batch updates on the fields of multiple documents within a file cabinet. The documents to be updated are specified by the `FileCabinetId` parameter.

To make a POST request to this endpoint, use the following URL: `{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{FileCabinetId}}/Operations/BatchDocumentsUpdateFields`.

The request should include the necessary parameters and data in the request body to specify the documents to be updated and the fields to be modified.

### Request Parameters

The request URL should include the following parameters:

- ServerUrl: The URL of the server hosting the file cabinet.
- Platform: The platform on which the file cabinet is hosted.
- FileCabinetId: The ID of the file cabinet containing the document.
    

The request body needs to contain an object in this format:

``` json
{
    "Source": {
        "$type": "BatchUpdateDialogExpressionSource",
        "Expression": {
            "Operation": "And",
            "Condition": [
                {
                    "DBName": "DOCUMENT_TYPE",
                    "Value": [
                        "Test"
                    ]
                }
            ]
        }
    },
    "Data": {
        "Field": [
            {
                "FieldName": "DOCUMENT_TYPE",
                "Item": "Batch Update Test"
            }
        ],
        "StoreDialogId": "",
        "BatchSize": "100",
        "BreakOnError": false,
        "ForceUpdate": true
    }
}

 ```

This object specifies the parameters to be used when performing a batch update on multiple documents in DocuWare. Here are the details for each field in the object:

- "Source" is an object that specifies the source documents to be updated using a Dialog Expression. Here are the details for each field in the object:
    
    - "$type" is a string that specifies the type of source documents being updated.
        
    - "Expression" is an object that specifies the search criteria to be used when selecting documents to be updated. Here are the details for each field in the object:
        
        - "Operation" is a string that specifies the boolean operation to be used when evaluating the search conditions in the "Condition" array. In this case, the value "And" indicates that the query should return results that match all of the specified search conditions.
            
        - "Condition" is an array of objects that specifies the search conditions to be used for the select list. Each object in the array represents a separate search condition, and contains the following fields:
            
            - "DBName" is a string that represents the name of the database field being searched.
                
            - "Value" is an array of strings that represents the search value(s) to be used for the specified database field.
                
- "Data" is an object that specifies the updated data to be applied to each of the source documents. Here are the details for each field in the object:
    
    - "Field" is an array of objects that represents the updated field data to be applied to each of the source documents. Each object in the array contains the following fields:
        
        - "FieldName" is a string that represents the name of the field to be updated.
            
        - "Item" is a string that represents the new value to be assigned to the field.
            
    - "StoreDialogId" is a string that represents the ID of the store dialog to be used when updating the documents.
        
    - "BatchSize" is a string that represents the maximum number of documents to be updated in a single batch.
        
    - "BreakOnError" is a boolean flag that indicates whether to stop the batch update process if an error is encountered.
        
    - "ForceUpdate" is a boolean flag that indicates whether to force the update of all documents, even if no changes were made to their field data.
        

### Response

The response to a successful execution of this request will have a status code of 200. The response body will contain the following information:

```
{
    "Item": [],
    "SuccessCount": 0
}

 ```

The `Item` field will contain an array of the updated documents, if any. In this case, the array is empty, indicating that no documents were updated. The `SuccessCount` field indicates the number of documents that were successfully updated.

Please refer to the API documentation for more details on the request parameters and the expected format of the request body.

request.description:
This HTTP POST request is used to update the fields of multiple documents in a file cabinet. The request should be made to the endpoint `{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{FileCabinetId}}/Operations/BatchDocumentsUpdateFields`.

### Request Parameters

The request URL should include the following parameters:

- ServerUrl: The URL of the server hosting the file cabinet.
- Platform: The platform on which the file cabinet is hosted.
- FileCabinetId: The ID of the file cabinet containing the document.
    

The request body needs to contain an object in this format:

``` json
{
    "DocId": [
        309,
        310
    ],
    "Keyword": [
        "Value1",
        "Value2"
    ],
    "FieldName": "ORDER_NUMBER",
    "BreakOnError": false,
    "StoreDialogId": "",
    "ForceUpdate": true
}

 ```

This object specifies the parameters to be used when performing a batch update on multiple documents in DocuWare. Here are the details for each field in the object:

- "DocId" is an array of integers that represents the document IDs of the documents to be updated.
- "Keyword" is an array of strings that represents the keywords of the documents to be updated.
- "FieldName" is a string that represents the name of the field to be updated.
- "BreakOnError" is a boolean flag that indicates whether to stop the batch update process if an error is encountered.
- "StoreDialogId" is a string that represents the ID of the store dialog to be used when updating the documents.
- "ForceUpdate" is a boolean flag that indicates whether to force the update of all documents, even if no changes were made to their field data.
    

### Response

The response from the execution of this request has a status code of 200, indicating a successful operation. The response body contains an "Item" array, which includes information about the updated documents. Each item in the array has a "Document" object, which represents a document in the file cabinet. The "Document" object contains the following properties:

- "FileCabinetId": The ID of the file cabinet to which the document belongs.
- "Fields": An array of field objects representing the updated fields of the document. Each field object has the following properties:
    - "SystemField": A boolean indicating whether the field is a system field.
    - "FieldName": The name of the field.
    - "FieldLabel": The label of the field.
    - "IsNull": A boolean indicating whether the field value is null.
    - "ReadOnly": A boolean indicating whether the field is read-only.
    - "Item": The value of the field.
    - "ItemElementName": The element name of the field value.

The "Document" object also has a "Flags" object, which contains various flags indicating the properties of the document. The available flags are:

- "IsCold": A boolean indicating whether the document is cold.
- "IsDBRecord": A boolean indicating whether the document is a database record.
- "IsCheckedOut": A boolean indicating whether the document is checked out.
- "IsCopyRightProtected": A boolean indicating whether the document is copy-right protected.
- "IsVoiceAvailable": A boolean indicating whether the document has voice available.
- "HasAppendedDocuments": A boolean indicating whether the document has appended documents.
- "IsProtected": A boolean indicating whether the document is protected.
- "IsDeleted": A boolean indicating whether the document is deleted.
- "IsEmail": A boolean indicating whether the document is an email.
    

The "Document" object also has a "Version" object, which represents the version of the document. It has the following properties:

- "Major": The major version number.
- "Minor": The minor version number.
    

Other properties of the "Document" object include:

- "Sections": An array of section objects representing the sections of the document.
- "ContentType": The content type of the document.
- "HaveMoreTotalPages": A boolean indicating whether there are more total pages in the document.
- "HasTextAnnotation": A boolean indicating whether the document has text annotations.
- "HasXmlDigitalSignatures": A boolean indicating whether the document has XML digital signatures.
- "AnnotationsPreview": A boolean indicating whether the document has an annotations preview.
- "TotalPages": The total number of pages in the document.
- "Id": The ID of the document.
- "LastModified": The last modified date of the document.
- "LastModifiedSpecified": A boolean indicating whether the last modified date is specified.
- "CreatedAt": The creation date of the document.
- "CreatedAtSpecified": A boolean indicating whether the creation date is specified.
- "FileSize": The size of the document file.
- "SectionCount": The number of sections in the document.
- "IntellixTrust": The IntellixTrust of the document.
- "VersionStatus": The version status of the document.
    

The response also includes a "SuccessCount" property, which indicates the number of documents that were successfully updated.

Please note that the values of specific properties in the request or response have been omitted for security and privacy reasons.

- DocumentId: The ID of the document.
    

The request body needs to contain an object in this format:

``` json
{
    "SourceFileCabinetId": "{{DocumentTrayId}}",
    "Documents": [

            "Id": {{DocumentId}},
            "Fields": null
        }
    ],
    "KeepSource": true,
    "FillIntellix": false,
    "UseDefaultDialog": false
}

 ```

- "SourceFileCabinetId": A string value that represents the ID of the document tray from which the document is being transferred. It is populated with a placeholder {{DocumentTrayId}} that is expected to be replaced with the actual ID value during runtime.
- "Documents": An array of objects that represent the documents to be transferred. In this case, there is only one document being transferred, which is identified by its Id.
    - "Id": A numeric value that represents the ID of the document being transferred. It is populated with a placeholder {{DocumentId}} that is expected to be replaced with the actual ID value during runtime.
    - "Fields": In this example, this property is set to null, indicating that no changes to the document's metadata should be made during the transfer.
- "KeepSource": A boolean value that specifies whether the source document should be kept or deleted after the transfer. In this example, it is set to true, indicating that the source document should be kept.
- "FillIntellix": A boolean value that specifies whether Intellix should be used to automatically populate metadata fields for the transferred document. In this example, it is set to false, indicating that Intellix should not be used.
- "UseDefaultDialog": A boolean value that specifies whether the default dialog should be used to catalog the transferred document. In this example, it is set to false, indicating that the default
    

### Response

The response contains the updated index fields for the specified document.

request.description:
This API endpoint is used to delete a specific document from a file cabinet in the specified platform. The document is identified by its unique document ID, which is provided in the URL path.

To delete a document, send an HTTP DELETE request to the following endpoint:

```
{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{FileCabinetId}}/Documents/{{DocumentId}}

 ```

### Request Parameters

- `ServerUrl` (string): The URL of the server where the platform is hosted.
- `Platform` (string): The name or identifier of the platform.
- `FileCabinetId` (string): The ID of the file cabinet from which the document will be deleted.
- `DocumentId` (string): The ID of the document to be deleted.
    

### Response

The API will respond with a status code indicating the success or failure of the delete operation. In a successful execution, the response has the following details:

- Status: 200
- OK
    

Please note that the response may vary depending on the specific implementation of the API.

 ```

The request should include the necessary parameters and data required for the merge operation.

### Request Parameters

The request URL should include the following parameters:

- ServerUrl: The URL of the server hosting the file cabinet.
- Platform: The platform on which the file cabinet is hosted.
- DocumentTrayId: The ID of the document tray.
    

The request body needs to contain an object in this format:

``` json
{
    "Documents": [
        6,
        7
    ],
    "Operation": "Clip",
    "Force": false
}

 ```

This is a request body in JSON format used to merge documents in DocuWare using the ContentMerge method. It includes the following key-value pairs:

- "Documents": An array that contains the IDs of the documents to be merged. In this example, Documents contains the IDs of two documents: 6 and 7.
- "Operation": A string that specifies the type of merge operation to be performed. In this example, the value of Operation is "Clip", which means that the documents will be merged by clipping or cutting the pages from each document and merging them together.
- "Force": A boolean value that indicates whether conflicting settings such as page orientation and size between the documents should be overridden during the merge.  
    If set to true, conflicting settings will be overridden with values from the source document.  
    If set to false, conflicting settings will not be overridden and an error will be thrown if they are detected. In this example, Force is set to \`false

This API endpoint allows you to divide the content of a document in a specific file cabinet and document tray. The content division is performed by making an HTTP POST request to the following URL: `{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{DocumentTrayId}}/Operations/ContentDivide?DocId={{ClippedDocumentId}}`.

### Request Parameters

The request URL should include the following parameters:

- ServerUrl: The URL of the server hosting the file cabinet.
- Platform: The platform on which the file cabinet is hosted.
- DocumentTrayId: The ID of the document tray.
- MergedDocumentId: The document ID.
    

The request body needs to contain an object in this format:

``` json
{
    "Operation": "Unclip"
}

 ```

This is a request body in JSON format used to merge documents in DocuWare using the ContentDivide method. It includes the following key-value pairs:

- "Operation": A string that specifies the type of merge operation to be performed. In this example, the value of Operation is "Unclip", which means that the documents will be unclipped.
    

### Response

The execution of this request returns a response with a status code of 200, indicating a successful execution. The response body contains the following information:

- `Count`: An object that provides information about the count of items returned. It includes the properties `HasMore`, `ExceedLimit`, and `Value`.
- `Items`: An array of objects representing the divided content. Each item in the array contains the following properties:
    - `FileCabinetId`: The ID of the file cabinet to which the divided content belongs.
    - `Fields`: An array of objects representing the fields associated with the divided content. Each field object includes properties such as `SystemField`, `FieldName`, `FieldLabel`, `IsNull`, `ReadOnly`, `Item`, and `ItemElementName`.

    - `Links`: An array of objects representing the links associated with the divided content. Each link object includes properties such as `rel` and `href`.
    - `Sections`: An array of objects representing the sections of the divided content. Each section object includes properties such as `SignatureStatus`, `Pages`, `Thumbnails`, `Links`, `Id`, `ContentType`, `HaveMorePages`, `PageCount`, `FileSize`, `OriginalFileName`, `ContentModified`, `HasTextAnnotation`, and `AnnotationsPreview`.
    - `ContentType`: The content type of the divided content.

    - `HasTextAnnotation`: A boolean value indicating whether the divided content has text annotations.

    - `TotalPages`: The total number of pages in the divided content.
    - `Id`: The ID of the divided content.
    - `Title`: The title of the divided content.
    - `LastModified`: The timestamp when the divided content was last modified.

    - `FileSize`: The file size of the divided content.
    - `SectionCount`: The number of sections in the divided content.

    - `VersionStatus`: The version status of the divided content.

Please note that specific values, such as names or emails, are not included in the response for security and privacy reasons. The response provides comprehensive information about the divided content and its associated properties.

request.description:
This API endpoint allows you to retrieve the stamps associated with a specific file cabinet. The stamps are used to add visual elements to documents, such as signatures, form fields, and custom fields.

To retrieve the stamps, you need to send an HTTP GET request to the following URL:

```
{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{FileCabinetId}}/Stamps

 ```

The response will be a JSON object with a `Stamp` property, which is an array of stamp objects. Each stamp object represents a unique stamp and contains the following properties:

- `Type`: The type of the stamp.
- `FormField`: An array of form fields associated with the stamp.
- `Fields`: An array of custom fields associated with the stamp.
- `HeadFont`: The font settings for the stamp's header.
- `Item`: The type of the stamp item.
- `Links`: An array of links associated with the stamp.
- `PasswordProtected`: Indicates whether the stamp is password protected.
- `Signature`: The signature associated with the stamp.
- `Color`: The color of the stamp.
- `Name`: The name of the stamp.
- `DisplayName`: The display name of the stamp.
- `Overwrite`: Indicates whether the stamp should overwrite existing content.
- `Id`: The unique identifier of the stamp.
- `IsHidden`: Indicates whether the stamp is hidden.
- `Autosave`: The autosave setting for the stamp.
- `CloseAfterStampSet`: Indicates whether the stamp should be closed after being set.
- `AutomaticallyOpenNextDocument`: Indicates whether the next document should be automatically opened after setting the stamp.
- `KeepStampActive`: Indicates whether the stamp should remain active after being set.
    

Please note that the response provided is an example and the actual response may vary based on the specific stamps associated with the file cabinet.

request.description:
This API endpoint allows you to add an annotation to a specific document in a file cabinet. To add an annotation, you need to make an HTTP POST request to the following URL: `{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{FileCabinetId}}/Documents/{{DocumentId}}/Annotation`.

### Request Parameters

The request URL should include the following parameters:

- `ServerUrl`: The URL of the server hosting the file cabinet.
- `Platform`: The platform on which the file cabinet is hosted.
- `FileCabinetId`: The ID of the file cabinet containing the document.
- `DocumentId`: The ID of the document.
    

The request body needs to contain an annotation object in this format:

``` json
{
    "Annotations": [
        {
            "PageNumber": 0,
            "SectionNumber": 0,
            "AnnotationsPlacement": {
                "Items": [
                    {
                        "$type": "StampPlacement",
                        "Location": {
                            "X": "100",
                            "Y": "100"
                        },
                        "StampId": "{{StampId}}",
                        "Layer": 1,
                        "Field": [
                            {
                                "Name": "<#1>",
                                "TypedValue": {
                                    "Item": "september",
                                    "ItemElementName": "string"
                                },
                                "Value": "september",
                                "TextAsString": "september"
                            }
                        ],
                        "Password": null
                    }
                ]
            }
        }
    ]
}

 ```

It contains a single `Annotations` array that stores information about annotations that will be added. In this example, there is only one annotation.

- `"Annotations"` is an array that specifies information about the annotations that will be added to a document. In this example, there is only one annotation being added.
- `"PageNumber"` specifies the page number of the document where the annotation will be placed. In this case, it is 0 (the first page).
- `"SectionNumber"` specifies the section of the document where the annotation will be placed. In this example, it is 0 (the first section).
- `"AnnotationsPlacement"` is an object that specifies the placement of the annotation on a document. It has an `Items` array that contains information about the annotation being placed.
- `"Items"` is an array that contains the actual information about the annotation. In this example, there is only one item.
- `"$type"` specifies the type of placement being used for the annotation. In this case, it is a `"StampPlacement"`.
- `"Location"` contains an `X` and `Y` value that specifies the position of the annotation on the page.
- `"StampId"` specifies the ID of the stamp that will be used as the annotation. This ID is typically generated by DocuWare.
- `"Layer"` specifies the annotation layer that the stamp will be placed on. In this example, it is layer 1.
- `"Field"` specifies information about the fields that will be added to the annotation. In this case, there is only one field.
- `"Name"` specifies the name of the field that will be added. In this example, it is `"<#1>"`.
- `"TypedValue"` specifies the type of value that will be added to the field. In this case, it is a string with the value `"september"`.
- `"Value"` specifies the value that will be added to the field. In this case, it is `"september"`.
- `"TextAsString"` specifies the text that will be added to the field. In this case, it is also `"september"`.
- `"Password"` if the stamp is protected by a password, the password needs to go here.
    

### Response

After making the request, the server will return a response with a status code of 200 if the annotation was successfully added. The response body will contain the details of the added annotation in JSON format.

Here is an example of the response body:

``` json
{
  "Annotations": [
    {
      "Annotation": [
        {
          "Items": [
            {
              "$type": "",
              "PngData": "",
              "DpiX": 0,
              "DpiY": 0,
              "Location": {
                "Left": 0,
                "Top": 0,
                "Width": 0,
                "Height": 0
              },
              "HeadFont": {
                "FontName": "",
                "Bold": true,
                "Italic": true,
                "Underlined": true,
                "StrikeThrough": true,
                "FontSize": 0,
                "Spacing": 0

              "Signature": "",

              "Frame": true,
              "ShowUser": true,
              "ShowDate": true,
              "ShowTime": true,
              "Created": {
                "User": "",
                "Time": ""
              },
              "Color": "",
              "Rotation": 0,
              "Transparent": true,
              "StrokeWidth": 0,
              "Id": ""
            }
          ],
          "Id": 0
        }
      ],
      "SectionNumber": 0,
      "PageNumber": 0

  ]
}

request.description:
This HTTP POST request is used to query the Trash Bin on the specified platform. The request is sent to the endpoint `{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/TrashBin/Query`.

### Request Parameters

The request URL should include the following parameters:

- ServerUrl: The URL of the server hosting the file cabinet.
- Platform: The platform on which the file cabinet is hosted.
    

The request body needs to contain an object in this format:

``` json
{
    "CompleteCondition": null,
    "Condition": [],
    "SortOrder": [
        {
            "Field": "DELETEDATETIME",
            "Direction": "Desc"
        }
    ],
    "ForceRefresh": true,
    "Operation": "And",
    "AdditionalResultFields": [],
    "Start": 0,
    "Count": 100,
    "IncludeSuggestions": false
}

 ```

This is a JSON request body used to query the Trash Bin in DocuWare through the {{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/TrashBin/Query endpoint. It includes the following key-value pairs:

- "CompleteCondition": A null value indicating that there is no complete condition specified.
- "Condition": An empty array that specifies the conditions that the documents in the Trash Bin must meet in order to be included in the results. In this case, there are no conditions specified.
- "SortOrder": An array that specifies the order in which the results should be sorted. The results will be sorted first by the DELETEDATETIME field in descending order (most recent first).
- "Field": A string value representing the name of the field to sort by. In this case, "Field" is set to "DELETEDATETIME".
- "Direction": A string value representing the direction of the sort. In this case, "Direction" is set to "Desc" for descending order.
- "ForceRefresh": A boolean value that indicates whether the server should ignore cached data and perform a fresh search. In this example, it is set to true to force a refresh.
- "Operation": A string value that specifies how the conditions specified in "Condition" should be combined. In this example, it is set to "And", indicating a logical AND operation.
- "AdditionalResultFields": An empty array that specifies additional document fields that should be included in the results. In this example, there are no additional result fields specified.
- "Start": A numeric value representing the index of the first result to return. In this example, it is set to 0 to begin with the first result.
- "Count": A numeric value representing the maximum number of results to return. In this example, it is set to 100 to limit the results to 100.
- "IncludeSuggestions": A boolean value that indicates whether the server should include suggestions for the query. In this example, it is set to false to exclude suggestions.
    

### Response

The response for the execution of this request has a status code of 200, indicating a successful response. The response body includs the following information:

- `TimeStamp`: The timestamp of the response.
- `Count`: An object containing information about the count of items in the Trash Bin.
    - `HasMore`: A boolean value indicating if there are more items in the Trash Bin.
    - `ExceedLimit`: A boolean value indicating if the count of items exceeds a limit.
    - `Value`: The count value of items in the Trash Bin.
- `Headers`: An array of objects representing the headers of the response.
    - `FieldName`: The name of the header field.
    - `Type`: The type of the header.
    - `Label`: The label of the header.
- `Rows`: An array containing the rows of data returned by the query. Each row is represented as an array of values.
- `Links`: An array of objects representing links related to the response.
    - `rel`: The relationship of the link.
    - `href`: The URL of the link.
    - `type`: The type of the link.

To restore multiple items, make a POST request to the following URL:

```
