
### Request Parameters

The request URL should include the following parameters:

- ServerUrl: The URL of the server hosting the file cabinet.
- Platform: The platform on which the file cabinet is hosted.
    

The request body needs to contain an object in this format:

``` json
{
    "Id": [

    ]
}
 ```

This is a JSON request body used to restore one or more deleted documents from the Trash Bin in DocuWare through the {{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/TrashBin/BatchRestore endpoint. It includes the following key-value pairs:

- "Id": An array of string values representing the IDs of the documents to be restored. In this example, the array contains one document ID: "tr_e44651fb-bcd8-4606-9fcf-f380a34e6f8d-484".  
    The "Id" array can include multiple document IDs if you want to restore multiple documents in a batch. Note that in order to restore a document, the corresponding file cabinet and organization must still exist and the user must have sufficient permissions to perform the operation.
    

### Response

``` json
{

}
 ```

``` json
            "Value": "Key 2 Value"
        }

}

 ```

The "DocumentApplicationProperty" array can include multiple key-value pairs if you want to update multiple custom document application properties at once. Note that any existing custom document application properties not included in the request will remain unchanged.  
Custom document application properties can be used to store additional information about a document beyond the standard metadata fields in DocuWare. They can also be used to implement custom workflow and business logic.

Make sure to include the necessary authentication headers and provide the required permissions to access and modify the document application properties.

request.description:
This API endpoint allows you to retrieve the sections of a specific file cabinet document.

To make a request, send an HTTP GET request to the following URL:

```
{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{FileCabinetId}}/Sections?DocId={{DocumentId}}

 ```

Replace `{{ServerUrl}}` with the base URL of the server, `{{Platform}}` with the platform name, `{{FileCabinetId}}` with the ID of the file cabinet, and `{{DocumentId}}` with the ID of the document you want to retrieve the sections for.

The response will have a status code of 200 if the request is successful. The response body will contain an array of sections, each represented by an object with the following properties:

- `SignatureStatus` (array): An array of signature statuses for the section.
- `Pages` (object): An object containing information about the pages of the section.
- `Thumbnails` (object): An object containing information about the thumbnails of the section.
- `Links` (array): An array of links related to the section.
- `Id` (string): The ID of the section.
- `ContentType` (string): The content type of the section.
- `HaveMorePages` (boolean): Indicates whether there are more pages in the section.
- `PageCount` (number): The total number of pages in the section.
- `FileSize` (number): The size of the section file.
- `OriginalFileName` (string): The original file name of the section.
- `ContentModified` (string): The date and time when the section content was last modified.
- `HasTextAnnotation` (boolean): Indicates whether the section has text annotations.
- `AnnotationsPreview` (boolean): Indicates whether the section has an annotations preview.
    

Please note that the specific values in the response may vary and should not be relied upon for documentation purposes.

request.description:
This endpoint retrieves information about a specific section of a document within a file cabinet on the specified platform.

## Request

### HTTP Request Method

GET

### URL

`{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{FileCabinetId}}/Sections/{{SectionId}}`

### Request Body

This request does not require a request body.

## Response

### Status

The request returns a status code of 200 if successful.

### Response Body

The response body contains the following information:

- `SignatureStatus`: An array indicating the signature status.
- `Pages`: An object containing an array of pages and links related to the pages.
- `Thumbnails`: An object containing an array of thumbnails and links related to the thumbnails.
- `Links`: An array of links related to the section.
- `Id`: The ID of the section.
- `ContentType`: The content type of the section.
- `HaveMorePages`: A boolean indicating if there are more pages available.
- `PageCount`: The count of pages in the section.
- `FileSize`: The size of the file.
- `OriginalFileName`: The original file name.
- `ContentModified`: The modified date of the content.
- `HasTextAnnotation`: A boolean indicating if the section has text annotation.
- `AnnotationsPreview`: A boolean indicating if annotations preview is available.
    

## Example

``` json
{
    "SignatureStatus": [""],
    "Pages": {
        "Page": [],
        "Links": [
            {
                "rel": "",
                "href": ""
            }
        ]
    },

}

 ```

request.description:
This API endpoint is used to download a file from a specific document in a file cabinet. The file is downloaded in the format specified by the "TargetFileType" parameter.

To download a file, make a GET request to the following URL:

```
{{ServerUrl}}/{{Platform}}/FileCabinets/{{FileCabinetId}}/Documents/{{DocumentId}}/FileDownload?TargetFileType=Auto&KeepAnnotations=false

 ```

The request should include the following parameters:

- `ServerUrl`: The URL of the server hosting the file cabinet.
- `Platform`: The platform on which the file cabinet is hosted.
- `FileCabinetId`: The ID of the file cabinet containing the document.
- `DocumentId`: The ID of the document from which the file should be downloaded.
- `TargetFileType` (optional): The desired file format of the downloaded file. If not specified, the file will be downloaded in the default format.
- `KeepAnnotations` (optional): A boolean value indicating whether to keep annotations in the downloaded file. If set to `false`, annotations will be removed from the file. If not specified, annotations will be kept in the file.
    

The response to this request will be the downloaded file. The response status code will be 200 if the file was successfully downloaded.

Please note that the response provided in the execution is null, which means that no specific information about the downloaded file is available. The content of the downloaded file will vary depending on the document and file cabinet configuration.

The response to this request will have a status code of 200, indicating a successful request. The response will be in XML format and will contain the workflow document history. However, the provided response does not contain any specific information about the workflow history.

Please note that this endpoint requires proper authentication and authorization to access the workflow history of a document.
